input.currentText: [fixture] I think we both agree on the BC and Sweden data showing carbon-tax effectiveness; the open question is whether that generalizes. What if both hold — they work where enforcement is stable, and the open question is which dominates by 2030? I think we are mostly aligned; the remaining piece is the timing question. Two questions tangled here: an empirical one (the effect size) and a values one (whether the tradeoff is acceptable). To settle the empirical part, we would need a primary record of the enforcement dates — that is the next evidence. What is still open is the generalization and the value weighting.
input.parentText: [fixture] A debate over whether carbon taxes reduce emissions generally.
input.threadContextExcerpt: [fixture] Debate room: carbon-tax effectiveness across jurisdictions.
